gaps: No native cancel API: OpenArt does not expose a cancel endpoint for in-flight generations. Once submitted, credits are consumed regardless. The cost-preview command (cost estimate) is the workaround.

Reverse-engineered API: OpenArt has no public API. The CLI relies on the unofficial /suite/api/* surface and Clerk session cookies. Frontend redeploys could break commands. Mitigation: re-import cookies, refresh model catalog with openart-pp-cli sync.

novel_features.name: Cost preview before submit
novel_features.description: Project the credit cost of a generation before you spend.
novel_features.rationale: Local models table joined with optional /suite/api/topaz/estimate; OpenArt's UI does not show projected cost for batches.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before any batch submit to avoid burning credits on a misconfigured run.

novel_features.name: Credit burn breakdown
novel_features.description: Aggregate credit spend by model, tool, day, or project.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local SQL over the synced credits_ledger; the OpenArt web UI shows entries serially with no aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'where did my credits go?' so you can shift work to cheaper models.

novel_features.name: Credit runway forecast
novel_features.description: Project how many weeks of runway your current balance gives at recent burn.
novel_features.rationale: Joins rolling 4-week burn from local credits_ledger against current free_credit_balance; UI shows neither rate nor projection.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces 'this paid plan covers another 6 weeks' so you can decide when to top up or slow down.

novel_features.name: Cheapest-model lookup
novel_features.description: Find the cheapest model that satisfies a target shape (type/duration/resolution/features).
novel_features.rationale: Pure local query against the models catalog ranked by credits_per_unit_default; OpenArt UI buries cost behind per-model tooltips.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents try a prompt on Grok Imagine for 150 credits before committing to Seedance 2's 800.

novel_features.name: Cross-model fan-out compare
novel_features.description: Run one prompt across multiple models in parallel; return a side-by-side report with cost and URLs.
novel_features.rationale: Submits N parallel form posts and joins results with the local models catalog; no other tool spans OpenArt's underlying model mix.
novel_features.example: openart-pp-cli compare --prompt "a phoenix in molten gold" --models byte-plus-seedance-2,kling2-6,grok-imagine --duration 5 --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Decide which model deserves the long Seedance shoot before committing.

novel_features.name: Replay a past generation
novel_features.description: Re-run a past generation, optionally on a different model, with parameter bumps.
novel_features.rationale: Reads media.input from local store and remaps params across model schemas; re-issues real submit. Single-pool tools (Replicate/Fal) cannot do cross-model remap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Iterate on past wins without retyping prompts; A/B test models cheaply.

novel_features.name: Prompt FTS recovery search
novel_features.description: Full-text search prior generations by prompt text with OpenArt-specific filters (model, audio, duration, starred, since).
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 over media_fts joined to per-resource metadata; OpenArt's media library has no prompt search.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Recover the prompt you wrote three weeks ago instead of paying to recreate it.

novel_features.name: Prompt spend leaderboard
novel_features.description: Rank past prompts by total credit spend over a window.
novel_features.rationale: Joins media.prompt x credits_ledger.amount via resource id; OpenArt UI never aggregates per-prompt across jobs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surface 'I have spent 8k credits iterating this dragon prompt' before iterating again.

novel_features.name: Library stats blob
novel_features.description: One-shot stats over the local media library: counts per type/model/period and rolling spend.
novel_features.rationale: Single SQL query across media + credits_ledger; pure local aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: A 'where am I' snapshot for both human users and agents starting a session.

narrative.headline: Spend your OpenArt credits programmatically. Generate videos with Seedance, Kling, Veo and friends from the terminal — async, agent-native, with offline credit accounting no other tool gives you.
narrative.value_prop: OpenArt has no public API. This CLI reverse-engineers the suite to give you fire-and-forget video generation, cross-model A/B comparisons, full-text prompt recovery, and credit-spend analytics that the OpenArt web UI does not surface. Your credits, your account, your terminal.
narrative.auth_narrative: Auth is your existing OpenArt browser session. Run `openart-pp-cli auth login --chrome` once to import the Clerk session cookies from your logged-in Chrome profile. No API key. If the session expires, log back into openart.ai in Chrome and re-import.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI is on PATH. Then run 'openart-pp-cli auth login --chrome' (interactive, requires Chrome) to import your OpenArt session cookies.
See every video model the CLI knows about, ordered by the catalog. No auth needed.
See what a Seedance run will actually cost before you spend. Pure local lookup, no auth needed.
Preview the request that would be submitted without spending credits. Drop --dry-run + add --wait --download ./videos/ to actually generate.
After running 'openart-pp-cli sync' to populate the local store, see where your last week of credits actually went.

troubleshoots.symptom: 401 / 403 from any command after a few days idle
troubleshoots.fix: Open openart.ai in Chrome, sign back in if prompted, then run `openart-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to refresh the imported cookies.

troubleshoots.symptom: `video gen --wait` exits before the video URL is populated
troubleshoots.fix: Increase the timeout: `--timeout 30m`. Seedance 2 at 1080p/15s commonly takes 12-15 minutes.

troubleshoots.symptom: `compare` reports one model as failed but others succeed
troubleshoots.fix: Check `openart-pp-cli media get <resourceId>` for the failed entry — most failures are model-specific (audio not supported, duration out of range).

troubleshoots.symptom: Cost estimate disagrees with the OpenArt UI
troubleshoots.fix: Run `openart-pp-cli sync` to refresh the local models catalog; OpenArt updates per-model pricing periodically.
Make sure Chrome is fully closed (cmd-Q) before running it — Chrome locks the cookie database while running. Re-open Chrome to use the site after import.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for openart-pp-cli when you have an OpenArt account and want to spend credits without sitting in a browser tab. Best for batched video prototyping with Seedance/Kling/Veo, scripted A/B tests across models, and recovering past prompts. Skip it when you want to do single ad-hoc generations interactively — the OpenArt web UI is fine for that.

recipes.title: Generate two Seedance videos and download them
recipes.command: openart-pp-cli video gen --prompt "a phoenix soaring over molten gold canyons" --model byte-plus-seedance-2 --duration 10 --count 2 --wait --download ./out/
recipes.explanation: The --wait flag polls every 5 seconds until both resources finish; --download streams the CDN MP4s to disk. Cost is shown before submit.

recipes.title: Compare a prompt across three models in parallel
recipes.command: openart-pp-cli compare --prompt "neon koi swimming through clouds" --models byte-plus-seedance-2,kling2-6,grok-imagine --duration 5 --agent --select results.model,results.credits,results.url
recipes.explanation: Fans out three parallel submits, polls all to completion, returns a markdown table. The --select narrowing keeps the agent payload small. Use --select on any list-shaped command.

recipes.title: Find the cheapest model that meets your shape
recipes.explanation: Ranks the video catalog by credits-per-video at 10s/720p. Pure local lookup — useful before submitting a non-precious prompt.

recipes.title: Audit weekly credit spend by model
recipes.explanation: Surfaces 'Seedance 2 ate 24,000 credits this week, Kling 1,400, Grok 600' so you can shift work toward cheaper models.

recipes.title: Submit a single Seedance video without waiting
recipes.explanation: Submits the gen and returns immediately with the historyId/resourceIds. Add --wait to block until completion (~10 min for Seedance 2). The terminal bell rings on completion when --wait is also set.

trigger_phrases: find that prompt I used on openart
